ingredients.item: 6 skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs (2¼ pounds, or 1 kg)
ingredients.original: 6 skin-on, bone-in chicken thighs (2¼ pounds, or 1 kg)

ingredients.item: 3¾ teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more if needed
ingredients.original: 3¾ teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more if needed

ingredients.item: 3 tablespoons Toum (or substitute 6 large cloves garlic, crushed)
ingredients.original: 3 tablespoons Toum (or substitute 6 large cloves garlic, crushed)

ingredients.item: 18 ounces (510 g) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
ingredients.original: 18 ounces (510 g) chickpeas, drained and rinsed

ingredients.item: 2 cups (400 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely
ingredients.original: 2 cups (400 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely

ingredients.item: 4 cups (960 ml) chicken broth or water
ingredients.original: 4 cups (960 ml) chicken broth or water

ingredients.item: 1 In a large bowl, season the chicken thighs with 1¼ teaspoons of the salt on both sides. Add the harissa, toum, and lemon juice and mix well to thoroughly coat the chicken. You can cover and let the chicken marinate for 1 to 2 hours in the refrigerator for optimal flavor, or use it right away.
ingredients.original: 1 In a large bowl, season the chicken thighs with 1¼ teaspoons of the salt on both sides. Add the harissa, toum, and lemon juice and mix well to thoroughly coat the chicken. You can cover and let the chicken marinate for 1 to 2 hours in the refrigerator for optimal flavor, or use it right away.

ingredients.item: 2 In a large, 2-inch (5 cm) deep skillet with a tight-fitting lid, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the chicken thighs, reserving any marinade, and sear them, skin sides down, for 2 to 3 minutes, until golden brown. Flip and sear on the other side for 2 to 3 minutes. Work in batches to avoid overcrowding if necessary, transferring the chicken to a plate. If there are any burnt seasonings in the skillet, remove them with a spoon but keep the oil.
ingredients.original: 2 In a large, 2-inch (5 cm) deep skillet with a tight-fitting lid, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the chicken thighs, reserving any marinade, and sear them, skin sides down, for 2 to 3 minutes, until golden brown. Flip and sear on the other side for 2 to 3 minutes. Work in batches to avoid overcrowding if necessary, transferring the chicken to a plate. If there are any burnt seasonings in the skillet, remove them with a spoon but keep the oil.

ingredients.item: 3 In the same skillet over medium heat, add the onion along with any reserved chicken marinade and cook, stirring often, until softened and translucent, 6 to 7 minutes.
ingredients.original: 3 In the same skillet over medium heat, add the onion along with any reserved chicken marinade and cook, stirring often, until softened and translucent, 6 to 7 minutes.

ingredients.item: 4 Add the spinach, chickpeas, and 1 teaspoon of the salt. Cook and stir until the spinach wilts, 2 to 3 minutes.
ingredients.original: 4 Add the spinach, chickpeas, and 1 teaspoon of the salt. Cook and stir until the spinach wilts, 2 to 3 minutes.

ingredients.item: 5 Add the rice and stir to combine with the chickpeas and vegetables. Add the chicken broth and the remaining 1½ teaspoons salt. Bring to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, carefully taste and adjust the salt if necessary; it should taste noticeably salty (see Note).
ingredients.original: 5 Add the rice and stir to combine with the chickpeas and vegetables. Add the chicken broth and the remaining 1½ teaspoons salt. Bring to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, carefully taste and adjust the salt if necessary; it should taste noticeably salty (see Note).

ingredients.item: 6 Add the chicken thighs back into the pan, arranging them on top of the rice. Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let cook, undisturbed, for 25 minutes. Remove the lid and fluff the rice using a fork.
ingredients.original: 6 Add the chicken thighs back into the pan, arranging them on top of the rice. Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let cook, undisturbed, for 25 minutes. Remove the lid and fluff the rice using a fork.

ingredients.item: 7 Serve warm with slaw or salad and yogurt.
ingredients.original: 7 Serve warm with slaw or salad and yogurt.
